creator: johann@egenetics.com
text:
Take two.  This sorts out the problems pointed out by sobomax.  There may be
more errors - this is my first attempt at a GNOME port.  Please advise me of
any further problems.

Thanks,
-- Johann


# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	pybliographer
#	pybliographer/Makefile
#	pybliographer/distinfo
#	pybliographer/pkg-comment
#	pybliographer/pkg-descr
#	pybliographer/pkg-plist
#	pybliographer/files
#	pybliographer/files/patch-Makefile.in
#
echo c - pybliographer
mkdir -p pybliographer > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - pybliographer/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >pybliographer/Makefile << 'END-of-pybliographer/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	pybliographer
X# Date created:		7 December 2000
X# Whom:	      		Johann Visagie <johann@egenetics.com>
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	pybliographer
XPORTVERSION=	1.0.7
XCATEGORIES=	misc python gnome
XMASTER_SITES=	${MASTER_SITE_GNOME}
XMASTER_SITE_SUBDIR=stable/sources/pyblio/
X
XMAINTAINER=	johann@egenetics.com
X
XBUILD_DEPENDS=	${PYTHON_SITELIBDIR}/_gnomemodule.so:${PORTSDIR}/x11-toolkits/py-gnome \
X		recode:${PORTSDIR}/converters/recode
XRUN_DEPENDS=	recode:${PORTSDIR}/converters/recode
X
XGNU_CONFIGURE=	yes
XUSE_GLIB=	yes
XUSE_PYTHON=	yes
XUSE_GNOME=	yes
XUSE_X_PREFIX=	yes
XCONFIGURE_ENV=	CPPFLAGS="-I${LOCALBASE}/include" LIBS="-L${LOCALBASE}/lib" \
X		Python="${PYTHON_CMD}"
XCONFIGURE_ARGS=	--with-included-gettext --with-recode-prefix="${LOCALBASE}"
X
XMAKEFILE_DIRS=	. doc/C doc/it
X
Xpost-extract:
X.for mdir in ${MAKEFILE_DIRS}
X	@ ${PERL} -pi -e \
X	  "s#\\$\(datadir\)/gnome#\\$\(datadir\)#" \
X	  ${WRKSRC}/${mdir}/Makefile.in
X.endfor
X
Xpost-install:
X	@ ${PYTHON_CMD} ${PYTHON_LIBDIR}/compileall.py \
X	  ${PREFIX}/share/gnome/${PORTNAME}
X	@ ${PYTHON_CMD} -O ${PYTHON_LIBDIR}/compileall.py \
X	  ${PREFIX}/share/gnome/${PORTNAME}
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-pybliographer/Makefile
echo x - pybliographer/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >pybliographer/distinfo << 'END-of-pybliographer/distinfo'
XMD5 (pybliographer-1.0.7.tar.gz) = bae69471d829dda99b453ca457925cd4
END-of-pybliographer/distinfo
echo x - pybliographer/pkg-comment
sed 's/^X//' >pybliographer/pkg-comment << 'END-of-pybliographer/pkg-comment'
XGUI and command-line tools for editing and searching bibliographic databases
END-of-pybliographer/pkg-comment
echo x - pybliographer/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >pybliographer/pkg-descr << 'END-of-pybliographer/pkg-descr'
XPybliographer is a tool for handling databases of bibliographic references.
XIt provides an homogeneous interface (with powerful editing and searching
Xfeatures) for several bibliographic database formats - currently supported
Xare BibTeX, Medline, Ovid and Refer.  In addition, it can export references
Xdirectly to LyX.
X
XBoth a scriptable command-line interface ("pybliographer") and a GNOME-
Xcompliant GUI ("pybliographic") are installed by the port.
X
XPybliographer is written in Python, and some Python knowledge would be
Xhelpful in making the most of the command-line environment and when doing
Xadvanced searches.  The GUI version, on the other hand, is extremely novice-
Xfriendly.
X
XAuthor:  Frederic Gobry <gobry@users.sourceforge.net>
XWWW:     http://www.gnome.org/pybliographer/
X
X-- Johann Visagie <johann@egenetics.com>
END-of-pybliographer/pkg-descr
echo x - pybliographer/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >pybliographer/pkg-plist << 'END-of-pybliographer/pkg-plist'
